what_it_does: Documents the grammar and schema of Claude Code session-store JSONL files: location/encoding, event taxonomy, compaction-boundary structure, fork/branch provenance, attribution/token fields, and a proposed projectable scan-row shape.
observations: Transcripts live at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl, one JSON event per line, append-only.
Compaction boundary is a PAIR: a system event with subtype==compact_boundary, immediately followed by a user event with isCompactSummary==true.
The lead session (bda8ce28) has exactly one real compaction boundary at line 8401; two other occurrences of continuation-like text were false positives inside tool results.
forkedFrom exists and links 1718 inherited events in the fork (11e7d395) to parent session bda8ce28; 630 events lack it and are the fork's own turns.
The Company's materialize_at_point clone tool differs from native fork by remapping uuids and using a constant forkedFrom.messageUuid.
Every assistant event carries .message.usage with real per-turn input/output/cache token counts and a model field.
A concrete scan-row schema is proposed with line, uuid, parentUuid, ts, type, role, attribution, model, token fields, content_bytes, is_tool_result, is_boundary, boundary_slug, gap_sec.
Work is split into reversible advisor-cleared items and [TIM-GATED] authority/registry/clone-fleet items.
Every claim is tagged with an evidence legend: [O]bserved, [I]nferred, or [V]erified.
The document format is marked provisional pending the co-designed ../COMMIT-GRAMMAR.md.
standouts: Structural-only compaction detection rule: never detect by text-grepping 'continued from' or 'compactMetadata'.
Corrected finding that forkedFrom does exist after an earlier 200-char-truncation misread.
Execution-verified claims using jq extraction and runtime/session_pointintime build_timeline/resolve_cut on live sources.
Flagged fidelity gap between native Claude Code fork and the Company's materialize_at_point emulation.
conventions: Uses a trust/author/date/verified header aligned with COMMIT-GRAMMAR.
Declares an owner lane and locked-scope status for the fork.
Grades evidence inline with [O]/[I]/[V] tags on every claim.
Logs corrections inline rather than silently overwriting.
Separates reversible analytics from authority-gated actions with explicit [TIM-GATED] labels.
Cross-references vision sections and other files heavily.
Includes status checklists for done, open, and gated items.
concerns: Document format is provisional and may be reshaped by the co-designed grammar.
Fork-detection partition is verified on only one fork; generalization awaits a second sample.
The 1M-window single-compaction causal link is marked inferred, not independently executed.
The 793 claude-fable-5 turns in the lead vs zero in the fork are unexplained.
Manual /compact semantics for compactMetadata remain unconfirmed.
notes: A fork-owned, execution-verified grammar doc for the session-store JSONL schema that insists on structural detection, corrects its own earlier misread, and keeps registry/authority work explicitly Tim-gated.
questions: What will the final COMMIT-GRAMMAR conventions require this document to change?
Is the 1M-token window the confirmed root cause of only one compaction, or still an inference?
Why does the lead session contain 793 claude-fable-5 assistant turns while the fork contains none?
Where is the docstring-referenced session with 41 compaction boundaries, since the live store contains none with >1 except the fork?
What exact fields does manual /compact populate in compactMetadata?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to define the session-store grammar and schema; the file does that and also reports verified findings, proposes a scan-row schema, and tracks open/gated work.
intention_signals: Codify the session store as a machine-readable source registry by defining structural event discriminators, a normalized scan row, and provenance rules, while reserving registry/authority commitments for Tim's direct approval.
novelty: Treats JSONL compaction boundaries as a paired structural event grammar, derives a projectable scan-row schema from live extraction, and flags a fidelity gap in the Company's own clone tool.
contribution: A verified structural grammar for session-store events and compaction boundaries; a concrete scan-row schema; a corrected forkedFrom provenance model; and an explicit split between reversible analytics and Tim-gated authority actions.
summary_for_embedding: A fork-owned schema document defining the Claude Code session-store JSONL grammar, including event taxonomy, structural compaction-boundary detection, fork provenance, token attribution, and a projectable scan-row shape, with claims graded observed/inferred/verified.
intention_for_embedding: To turn live session transcripts into a normalized, programmatic source registry for channel-memory tooling while separating reversible analytics from authority-gated commitments.
